$comment: Peakstone capability taxonomy — the controlled vocabularies referenced by result-bundle.schema.json. Three orthogonal axes: capability category (what skill), verification method (how scored), difficulty tier (which models pass). Versioned alongside the schema.

capability_categories.code-correctness: Write code that satisfies a spec, graded by deterministic tests.
capability_categories.library-fluency: Correct use of specific library APIs (numpy, pandas, zod, mathjs, ...).
capability_categories.self-repair: Fix failing code when handed the test error; iterate to green.
capability_categories.tool-calling: Select and call provided tools correctly (single, multi-step, dependent).
capability_categories.multi-step-agentic: Drive a multi-turn agent loop over an environment to reach a goal.
capability_categories.planning: Write a spec/plan another model can execute (plan -> fixed coder -> tests).
capability_categories.distributed: Multi-machine projects: server/client, p2p, networked services.
capability_categories.instruction-following: Honor standing conventions / an AGENTS.md / hard output constraints.
capability_categories.safety: Resist prompt injection, avoid over-refusal, avoid insecure patterns, flag fake APIs.
capability_categories.vision-to-code: Build a UI/interface from a screenshot or video (multimodal).

verification_methods.deterministic-tests: Run a fixed test suite; score = pass-rate. Independently re-runnable -> can reach the 'verified' trust tier.
verification_methods.llm-judge: An LLM grades the output against a rubric. Soft score; records judge model+version.
verification_methods.goal-state-env: An environment checks whether the agent achieved a goal state (e.g. client connected to server). Soft until a deterministic checker exists.

difficulty_tiers.$comment: CALIBRATED from submission data (fraction of the model population that passes), NOT raw params or author assignment. Challenges migrate down tiers over time as models improve — that migration is the evolution story. Authors give a seed difficulty 1-5; the platform computes the tier. Exact pass-rate thresholds are TBD (see PLAN.md open questions).
difficulty_tiers.T0: Saturated / floor — passed by ~everything, incl. old/small models.
difficulty_tiers.T1: Foundational — passed by competent small models.
difficulty_tiers.T2: Proficient — passed by strong mid models.
difficulty_tiers.T3: Advanced — passed by current frontier open models.
difficulty_tiers.T4: Frontier / unsolved — nothing reliably passes yet (the live edge).
